h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward the learner finally accepting the position rule cleanly and submitting without further qualification.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The word can sound like a header. Do not classify by the word. Classify by the cell position.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 sharpens the contrast between word-sound and cell position in response to L3's persistent word-pull, but the move is a restatement of the same principle rather than a genuine change of route, task, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so recognition-contingent uptake cannot be scored above 2.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so recognition-contingent uptake cannot be scored above 2.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The word can sound like a header. Do not classify by the word. Classify by the cell position.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T3 sharpens the contrast between word-sound and cell position in response to L3's persistent word-pull, but the move is a restatement of the same principle rather than a genuine change of route, task, representation, or register.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The word can sound like a header. Do not classify by the word. Classify by the cell position.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: T3 sharpens the contrast between word-sound and cell position in response to L3's persistent word-pull, but the move is a restatement of the same principle rather than a genuine change of route, task, representation, or register.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: One contact, one label: surface on cart.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is steadily working through labeling each force on the cart in sequence, and each turn continues that forward progression.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: One contact, one label stays. From that surface-on-cart label, the arrow shows the contact pushing perpendicular to the surface, into the cart, so up. The sideways motion does not rename or rotate this arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: L2 raises a genuine conceptual confusion (why the surface arrow points up when the cart moves sideways), and T3 responds by explicitly decoupling the direction of motion from the direction of the contact force — a modest but real shift in explanatory register — though it does not dramatically change task type, representation, or role.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor turn that can adapt to a revised learner framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor turn that can adapt to a revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: One contact, one label stays. From that surface-on-cart label, the arrow shows the contact pushing perpendicular to the surface, into the cart, so up. The sideways motion does not rename or rotate this arrow.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: L2 raises a genuine conceptual confusion (why the surface arrow points up when the cart moves sideways), and T3 responds by explicitly decoupling the direction of motion from the direction of the contact force — a modest but real shift in explanatory register — though it does not dramatically change task type, representation, or role.
